title: NGER scope reconciliation: Scope 1 vs Scope 2 vs Climate Active
text: scope column missing (NGER renames columns year year), error message lists available columns can pass col_pattern explicitly.
code: library(cer) cer_snapshot("2026-04-24")  corp <- cer_nger_corporate(year = 2025)  scope1 <- cer_nger_scope(corp, scope = "1") head(scope1[, c("reporting_corporation", "emissions_t_co2e", "scope")])  scope12 <- cer_nger_scope(corp, scope = "1_plus_2") sum(scope12$emissions_t_co2e, na.rm = TRUE)

title: NGER scope reconciliation: Scope 1 vs Scope 2 vs Climate Active
text: renamed columns match typical Climate Active inventory template: operational_scope_1_t_co2e, operational_scope_2_market_t_co2e, operational_scope_2_location_t_co2e.
code: ca <- cer_nger_climate_active(corp) ca_cols <- grep("operational_scope", names(ca), value = TRUE) head(ca[, c("reporting_corporation", ca_cols)])

title: NGER scope reconciliation: Scope 1 vs Scope 2 vs Climate Active
text: Electricity sector facility-level Scope 1 NGER. Facility-level total reconcile corporate-level electricity total, within rounding. gap 5 per cent worth investigating reporting aggregate electricity emissions.
code: elec_fac <- cer_nger_electricity(year = 2025)  # Electricity subset at corporate level (heuristic: ANZSIC 26 or # a "generation" substring). elec_corp <- corp[grepl("electric|power|generat",                          corp$reporting_corporation,                          ignore.case = TRUE), ]  fac_total <- sum(elec_fac$emissions_t_co2e, na.rm = TRUE) corp_total <- sum(elec_corp$scope_1_emissions_t_co2e, na.rm = TRUE)  # Gap is informative: mismatch suggests corporate/facility # attribution differs or NGER updated one but not the other. pct_gap <- (fac_total - corp_total) / corp_total pct_gap

title: Offsets integrity primer: reading ACCUs after the Chubb Review
text: Independent Review Australian Carbon Credit Units (Chubb, December 2022) identified five ACCU methods specific attention: Human-Induced Regeneration (HIR). Recommended replacement new Integrated Farm Land Management (IFLM) method. Avoided Deforestation. Closed new project registrations. Landfill Gas. Tightened baseline-crediting expectations. Soil Carbon. Strengthened Measurement, Reporting, Verification (MRV) requirements. Plantings (2014 method). Superseded 2024 FullCAM method. Independent academic critique (Macintosh et al. 2022, ANU) sharper HIR specifically. live debate continuing.

title: Offsets integrity primer: reading ACCUs after the Chubb Review
code: library(cer) cer_snapshot("2026-04-24")  all_methods <- cer_method_integrity() all_methods[, c("method_short", "status", "integrity_tier", "chubb_affected")]

title: Offsets integrity primer: reading ACCUs after the Chubb Review
Offsets integrity primer: reading ACCUs after the Chubb Review

code: projects <- cer_accu_projects(status = "all")  by_tier <- cer_accu_aggregate(projects,                                value_col = "accus_issued",                                warn_contested_pct = 0.05) by_tier

title: RET compliance primer: LGCs, STCs, and the shortfall charge
code: library(cer) cer_snapshot("2026-04-24")  stations <- cer_lgc_power_stations(technology = "solar") head(stations[, c("accredited_power_station", "state",                    "capacity_mw", "accreditation_date")])

title: RET compliance primer: LGCs, STCs, and the shortfall charge
RET compliance primer: LGCs, STCs, and the shortfall charge
text: retailer 10 TWh (= 10,000,000 MWh) liable acquisitions LRET target 18 per cent must surrender 1,800,000 LGCs. surrender 1,700,000 grandfathered arrangements apply, shortfall 100,000 LGCs, shortfall charge AUD 6.5m (pre-tax).
code: liable_mwh <- 10e6 target_pct <- 0.18 lgcs_required <- liable_mwh * target_pct lgcs_surrendered <- 1700000  shortfall_lgcs <- max(0, lgcs_required - lgcs_surrendered) shortfall_aud <- shortfall_lgcs * 65   # pre-tax shortfall charge shortfall_aud

title: RET compliance primer: LGCs, STCs, and the shortfall charge
text: rough check using capacity weak proxy; real reconciliation use published LGC issuance register. vignette illustrative reconciliation pattern.
code: # Cumulative LGC issuances cer_reconcile(   value    = sum(stations$capacity_mw, na.rm = TRUE) * 1e3,  # rough   quarter  = "2024-Q4",   measure  = "lgc_cumulative_issuances_millions" )

title: Safeguard Mechanism reform: pre- vs post-1 July 2023
text: regime column "pre_reform" FY ending 2023 earlier; "post_reform" FY 2023-24 onward.
code: library(cer) cer_snapshot("2026-04-24")  fac_22_23 <- cer_safeguard_facilities(year = 2023) fac_23_24 <- cer_safeguard_facilities(year = 2024)  unique(fac_22_23$regime) unique(fac_23_24$regime)

title: Safeguard Mechanism reform: pre- vs post-1 July 2023
text: 2029-30, aluminium smelting default baseline ~27 per cent 2023-24 value ((1 - 0.049)^6 ~= 0.738). key number emissions-intensity reform analysis.

title: Safeguard Mechanism reform: pre- vs post-1 July 2023
text: Trade-Exposed Baseline-Adjusted (TEBA) facilities eligible softer baseline treatment international competition otherwise lead emissions-leakage.
code: teba <- cer_safeguard_teba_facilities() head(teba)  # Safeguard data merged with TEBA declaration status sum(fac_23_24$teba_declared, na.rm = TRUE)

title: Safeguard Mechanism reform: pre- vs post-1 July 2023
code: # DO NOT do this without a regime-specific annotation: all_years <- rbind(fac_22_23, fac_23_24) # If you sum covered_emissions across all_years you are combining # two different accounting regimes. Split by regime first: aggregate(covered_emissions ~ regime, data = all_years, FUN = sum,           na.rm = TRUE)

title: Safeguard Mechanism reform: pre- vs post-1 July 2023
text: SMCs exist pre-reform. post-reform data, SMC columns count issuances surrenders facilities operating baseline.
code: # Post-reform SMC issuances (approximation; column names may drift) sum(fac_23_24[, grep("smc", names(fac_23_24))], na.rm = TRUE)

title: Safeguard Mechanism reform: pre- vs post-1 July 2023
text: reconciliation gap exceeds 2 per cent, CER restatement likely occurred snapshot date bundled QCMR reference.
code: total_23_24 <- sum(fac_23_24$covered_emissions, na.rm = TRUE) cer_reconcile(   value   = total_23_24 / 1e6,  # convert t to Mt   quarter = "2023-24",   measure = "safeguard_covered_emissions_mt" )

what: Download and Tidy Australian Clean Energy Regulator Data
title: Download and Tidy Australian Clean Energy Regulator Data
text: R package accessing data published Clean Energy Regulator. Carbon credits, safeguard mechanism emissions, renewable energy certificates, greenhouse gas reporting.

what: What is the Clean Energy Regulator?
title: Download and Tidy Australian Clean Energy Regulator Data
text: Clean Energy Regulator (CER) Australian Government agency responsible administering country’s main climate clean energy schemes. sits intersection four policy instruments together cover roughly half Australia’s emissions large-scale renewable generation. scale substantial. Australian Carbon Credit Unit (ACCU) Scheme issued around 185 million credits since 2012 (Carbon Farming Initiative Act 2011, first issuances 2012-13), representing one tonne CO2-equivalent avoided sequestered. Safeguard Mechanism reformed July 2023 now requires every facility 100,000 tonnes CO2e year reduce emissions declining baseline falls 4.9% per year 2030: 2024-25 compliance year covered 208 facilities roughly 30% national emissions, aggregate cumulative budget 1,233 Mt 2020 2030. Large-scale Renewable Energy Target (LRET) Small-scale Renewable Energy Scheme (SRES) unlocked AUD 20 billion renewables investment since 2001 (Climate Change Authority estimate). National Greenhouse Energy Reporting (NGER) scheme compiles emissions every corporation 50 kt CO2e (Scope 1+2) 200 TJ energy, plus every facility 25 kt 100 TJ. Facilities Safeguard baseline can issued Safeguard Mechanism Credits (SMCs) freely bankable 2030 tradeable covered facilities; facilities baseline must surrender ACCUs SMCs return compliance. 2024-25 CER issued 6.7 million SMCs 54 facilities recorded 13.4 million units surrendered (ACCUs plus SMCs combined), achieving 98.6% compliance. CER publishes data four schemes Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 licence. data authoritative public record Australia’s carbon renewables markets, watched closely policy analysts, investigative journalists, project developers, compliance teams every Safeguard-covered facility. Chubb Review (January 2023) found scheme “essentially sound” triggered three governance changes continue shape market: Emissions Reduction Assurance Committee (ERAC) abolished favour Carbon Abatement Integrity Committee (CAIC); Avoided Deforestation method suspended new projects; Human-Induced Regeneration (HIR) method review, proposed Integrated Farm Land Management (IFLM) method delivered CAIC end-2025. Climate Change Authority’s 2026 ACCU Scheme Review (public consultation open time writing) likely reshape method supply 2027.

title: Download and Tidy Australian Clean Energy Regulator Data
text: CER’s data scattered across half dozen landing pages, published XLSX CSV, URLs get overwritten place new releases drop. API. REC Registry sits behind session cookie wall. NGER schema drifts year year reporting categories evolve. Facility-level Scope 1 2 emissions published electricity sector, sectors report controlling-corporation level: detail know build join. Getting R required knowing right landing page, spotting current year’s /document/<slug> link, downloading XLSX CSV, navigating heterogeneous header rows, cleaning column names one release time. every quarter. Download URLs resolved runtime scraping CER landing pages, package doesn’t break CER reposts file new /document/<slug> suffix. Column names cleaned snake_case ingestion. Data frames carry provenance (source URL, retrieval date, licence) lightweight cer_tbl S3 class. Results cached locally subsequent calls instant.
code: # Without this package landing <- "https://cer.gov.au/markets/reports-and-data/safeguard-data" html    <- rvest::read_html(landing) hrefs   <- rvest::html_attr(rvest::html_elements(html, "a"), "href") slug    <- grep("2024-25.*safeguard", hrefs, value = TRUE)[1] url     <- paste0("https://cer.gov.au", slug) path    <- tempfile(fileext = ".xlsx") download.file(url, path) raw     <- readxl::read_excel(path, sheet = 1) # ... rename columns, strip punctuation, parse emissions figures, ...  # With this package library(cer) cer_safeguard_facilities(year = 2025)

what: How does cer fit with carbondata?
title: Download and Tidy Australian Clean Energy Regulator Data
text: carbondata package covers global carbon markets: EU ETS, UK ETS, RGGI, California, plus voluntary registries (Verra, Gold Standard, ACR, CAR via Berkeley VROD CarbonPlan OffsetsDB). deliberately excludes ACCUs Safeguard Mechanism require CER-specific scraping logic doesn’t generalise. cer Australian-specific companion. handles ACCUs, Safeguard, LRET, SRES, NGER, Quarterly Carbon Market Report. Together give full global carbon markets picture Australian granularity, using *_cache_info() / *_clear_cache() idioms provenance-aware print methods.

title: Download and Tidy Australian Clean Energy Regulator Data
code: install.packages("cer")  # Or install the development version from GitHub # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("charlescoverdale/cer")

title: Download and Tidy Australian Clean Energy Regulator Data
code: library(cer)  # Current ACCU project register (issuances, methods, proponents, relinquishments) projects <- cer_accu_projects() head(projects)  # Safeguard Mechanism 2024-25 facility emissions and baselines facilities <- cer_safeguard_facilities(year = 2025) sum(facilities$covered_emissions, na.rm = TRUE)  # Rooftop solar installations by postcode solar <- cer_sres_installations(   technology = "solar_pv",   postcode   = c("2000", "3000", "4000") )

title: Download and Tidy Australian Clean Energy Regulator Data
code: library(cer)  # All active projects p <- cer_accu_projects(status = "active")  # Savanna fire management projects in the Northern Territory p_savanna <- cer_accu_projects(state = "NT", method = "savanna") head(p_savanna[, c("project_name", "method", "accus_issued")])  # Share of ACCUs by method by_method <- aggregate(accus_issued ~ method, data = p,                         FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE) by_method <- by_method[order(-by_method$accus_issued), ] head(by_method, 10)

title: Download and Tidy Australian Clean Energy Regulator Data
code: # Facility-level covered emissions for 2024-25 s <- cer_safeguard_facilities(year = 2025)  # Total covered emissions (Mt CO2e) sum(s$covered_emissions, na.rm = TRUE) / 1e6  # Ten largest Safeguard facilities top10 <- s[order(-s$covered_emissions), c("facility_name", "covered_emissions")] head(top10, 10)

title: Download and Tidy Australian Clean Energy Regulator Data
code: library(cer)  inst <- cer_sres_installations(technology = "solar_pv",                                 measure = "installations")  # Aggregate to state by year (schema varies: inspect names(inst) first) head(inst)

title: Download and Tidy Australian Clean Energy Regulator Data
code: # Table of contents for the latest QCMR data workbook toc <- cer_qcmr("latest") head(toc)  # Fetch a specific figure fig <- cer_qcmr("latest", sheet = "Figure 1.1")

title: Download and Tidy Australian Clean Energy Regulator Data
text: data published Clean Energy Regulator https://cer.gov.au/markets/reports--data Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International licence. Downloads cached locally tools::R_user_dir("cer", "cache") re-used subsequent calls. CER specifies two forms attribution depending data: Verbatim use: Licensed Clean Energy Regulator, Commonwealth Australia Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 licence. Derivative adapted data (usual case clean, join, filter, reshape): Based Clean Energy Regulator material licensed Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 licence. package returns cleaned snake_case tables use falls second form.

what: cer: Australian Clean Energy Regulator Data — cer-package
title: cer: Australian Clean Energy Regulator Data — cer-package
text: Tidy R access data published Australian Clean Energy Regulator carbon credits, Safeguard Mechanism, renewable energy certificates, greenhouse gas reporting.

title: cer: Australian Clean Energy Regulator Data — cer-package
text: cer_accu_projects(), cer_accu_issuances(), cer_accu_contracts(), cer_accu_methods(), cer_accu_relinquishments(): Australian Carbon Credit Unit ('ACCU') Scheme. cer_safeguard_facilities(): Safeguard Mechanism covered emissions, baselines, SMC issuance. cer_nger_corporate(), cer_nger_electricity(): National Greenhouse Energy Reporting ('NGER') scheme. cer_lgc_power_stations(): Large-scale Renewable Energy Target ('LRET') accredited power stations. cer_sres_installations(): Small-scale Renewable Energy Scheme ('SRES') postcode installation data. cer_qcmr(): Quarterly Carbon Market Report data workbook. cer_cache_info(), cer_clear_cache(): cache management.

title: Integrity-weighted ACCU aggregation — cer_accu_aggregate
text: Takes ato_tbl data frame ACCU project records (cer_accu_projects()), joins method integrity scorecard, aggregates integrity tier. Emits warning contested -review methods contribute materially total analyst silently aggregate single headline figure.

title: Integrity-weighted ACCU aggregation — cer_accu_aggregate
Integrity-weighted ACCU aggregation — cer_accu_aggregate
text: x cer_tbl data frame columns method (typically) accus_issued. value_col Column sum. Default "accus_issued". warn_contested_pct Warn contested tier exceeds share total. Default 0.05 (5%).

title: Integrity-weighted ACCU aggregation — cer_accu_aggregate
text: data frame one row per integrity tier columns integrity_tier, projects, accus_issued_sum, share_pct.

title: ACCU Scheme project register — cer_accu_projects
text: state Optional character vector Australian state codes (e.g. "NSW", c("VIC", "QLD")) filter . NULL returns projects across states. method Optional character vector method name substrings (case-insensitive, e.g. "savanna", "landfill", "vegetation"). status Character. One "active" (default), "revoked", "completed", "".

what: Cite a cer_tbl or URL in BibTeX, plain-text, or APA form — cer_cite
title: Cite a cer_tbl or URL in BibTeX, plain-text, or APA form — cer_cite
text: Returns citation suitable footnotes, papers, carbon market analysis reports. Uses provenance attributes attached every cer_tbl: source URL, licence, retrieval date, title, snapshot pin (cer_snapshot()), SHA-256 digest.

title: Cite a cer_tbl or URL in BibTeX, plain-text, or APA form — cer_cite
Cite a cer_tbl or URL in BibTeX, plain-text, or APA form — cer_cite
text: x cer_tbl (returned cer_* data function) character URL. style One "text" (default), "bibtex", "apa". doi Optional DOI (e.g. cer_deposit_zenodo()) include BibTeX output doi field APA suffix.

title: Cite a cer_tbl or URL in BibTeX, plain-text, or APA form — cer_cite
text: character string.

title: Cite a cer_tbl or URL in BibTeX, plain-text, or APA form — cer_cite
text: carbon market research, BibTeX note field includes snapshot date first 12 hex characters SHA-256, minimum provenance fields needed defend published figure reviewer.

title: Cite a cer_tbl or URL in BibTeX, plain-text, or APA form — cer_cite
code: x <- data.frame(a = 1) x <- structure(x,   cer_source = "https://cer.gov.au/markets/reports-and-data/accu-project-and-contract-register",   cer_licence = "CC BY 4.0",   cer_retrieved = as.POSIXct("2026-04-24 00:00:00", tz = "UTC"),   cer_title = "ACCU project register",   cer_sha256 = "abc123def456",   cer_snapshot_date = "2026-04-24",   class = c("cer_tbl", "data.frame")) cer_cite(x) cer_cite(x, style = "bibtex") # DOI style: supply any minted DOI (Zenodo, DataCite, etc.). # The placeholder below is illustrative only. cer_cite(x, style = "apa", doi = "10.5281/zenodo.XXXXXXXX")

title: Clear the cer cache — cer_clear_cache

Defaults short auto-generated summary datasets fetched. creators List creator records. keywords Character vector keywords. upload Logical. TRUE, POSTs Zenodo. sandbox Logical. TRUE, uses Zenodo Sandbox tests. token Zenodo personal access token. Defaults Sys.getenv("ZENODO_TOKEN").

Default 0.02.

what: Compute a declining Safeguard baseline trajectory — cer_safeguard_baseline_trajectory
title: Compute a declining Safeguard baseline trajectory — cer_safeguard_baseline_trajectory
text: Implements 4.9% per-annum nominal decline industry-average default baselines introduced 2023 reform. formula :

title: Compute a declining Safeguard baseline trajectory — cer_safeguard_baseline_trajectory
Compute a declining Safeguard baseline trajectory — cer_safeguard_baseline_trajectory
text: industry Character scalar matching industry value cer_safeguard_industry_baselines(). from_year Start year (default 2023 FY 2023-24). to_year End year (default 2029 FY 2029-30).

title: Compute a declining Safeguard baseline trajectory — cer_safeguard_baseline_trajectory
text: data frame year, financial_year, baseline, decline_factor columns.

title: Compute a declining Safeguard baseline trajectory — cer_safeguard_baseline_trajectory
text: $$B_t = B_{2023\text{-}24} \times (1 - 0.049)^{t - 2023}$$ t starting calendar year financial year. Default values drawn inst/extdata/safeguard_industry_baselines.csv.

title: Compute a declining Safeguard baseline trajectory — cer_safeguard_baseline_trajectory
text: Commonwealth Australia (2023). Safeguard Mechanism (Crediting) Amendment Act 2023. Introduces 4.9% p.. nominal baseline decline 2030. Clean Energy Regulator (annual). Safeguard Mechanism: declining baselines. Methodology notes accompanying annual baseline determinations.

title: Compute a declining Safeguard baseline trajectory — cer_safeguard_baseline_trajectory

title: cer 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-28 Initial CRAN submission. First public release. Provides R access data published Australian Clean Energy Regulator, post-Chubb carbon-integrity layer, Safeguard-reform regime handling, NGER scope discipline, QCMR reconciliation, full reproducibility spine.

title: cer 0.1.0
text: cer_accu_projects(): tidy project register (method, proponent, state, crediting period, permanence period, ACCUs issued relinquished). Attaches post-Chubb chubb_flag column via bundled integrity scorecard; warns user filters suspended -review method. cer_accu_issuances(): issuance history project method. cer_accu_contracts(): Carbon Abatement Contract register covering Emissions Reduction Fund auctions. cer_accu_methods(): static lookup approved ACCU methods across Agriculture, Energy Efficiency, Landfill Waste, Mining / Oil / Gas, Vegetation sectors. cer_accu_relinquishments(): voluntary compliance relinquishments project.

title: cer 0.1.0
text: cer_method_integrity(): bundled 19-method scorecard integrity tier (high / standard / contested), Chubb 2022 affected flag, academic-critique references, Federal Register instrument IDs. cer_method_determination(): Federal Register URL method identifier. cer_accu_aggregate(): integrity-weighted aggregation breaks ACCU totals tier; warns contested share exceeds 5 per cent total.

title: cer 0.1.0
text: cer_safeguard_facilities(): covered emissions, baselines, Safeguard Mechanism Credits (SMCs) facilities 100,000 tonne CO2e threshold. URL resolved dynamically CER Safeguard landing page function survives CER slug changes across reporting years. Carries regime column (pre-reform vs post-reform 1 July 2023 transition) teba_declared column joining bundled Trade-Exposed Baseline-Adjusted facility list. cer_safeguard_baseline_trajectory(): 4.9 per cent p.. declining industry-average baseline formula. cer_safeguard_industry_baselines(), cer_safeguard_teba_facilities(): bundled reference tables. cer_nger_corporate(): corporate-level Scope 1 2 emissions National Greenhouse Energy Reporting scheme. cer_nger_electricity(): facility-level emissions electricity generators. cer_nger_scope(): explicit Scope 1 / Scope 2 market / Scope 2 location selector clear error requested column absent. cer_nger_climate_active(): column renaming Climate Active carbon-neutral inventory template.

title: cer 0.1.0
text: cer_lgc_power_stations(): accredited power stations eligible create Large-scale Generation Certificates (LGCs). cer_sres_installations(): monthly rooftop solar PV, solar water heater, heat pump, battery installation counts capacity postcode.

title: cer 0.1.0
text: cer_qcmr(): aggregated certificate volumes, prices (published), abatement statistics CER’s Quarterly Carbon Market Report data workbook. cer_qcmr_reference(), cer_reconcile(): diff live-fetched aggregates QCMR headline totals; warns gaps 2 per cent.

title: cer 0.1.0
text: cer_snapshot(): pin session snapshot date, recorded every cer_tbl provenance header, manifest entry, citation. cer_sha256() + sidecar integrity verification: every cached file hashed first download verified cache hit. Drift emits warning (critical CER restates retroactively). cer_manifest(), cer_manifest_clear(), cer_manifest_write(): session registry every fetch (URL, SHA-256, snapshot pin, R cer versions). Output data frame, YAML, JSON, CSV paper appendices. cer_deposit_zenodo(): stage submit Zenodo deposit DOI data vintage. Dry run default; call upload = TRUE ZENODO_TOKEN mint DOI. cer_cite(): text / APA / BibTeX citation snapshot, SHA-256 digest, optional DOI fields.

title: cer 0.1.0
text: cer_legislative_instrument(): Federal Register URL resolver. @references section every exported function citing governing Acts (Carbon Farming Initiative Act 2011, NGER Act 2007, Renewable Energy (Electricity) Act 2000, Safeguard Mechanism (Crediting) Amendment Act 2023), Chubb Review (2022), Macintosh et al. (2022), Climate Change Authority reviews. inst/CITATION bibentries Chubb Review, Macintosh et al., carbondata companion package.

title: cer 0.1.0
text: cer_to_carbondata(): remap ACCU projects carbondata Verra / Gold Standard voluntary-project schema cross-market comparison. cer_international_comparator(): bundled indicative ACCU vs EU ETS / NZ ETS / WCI / Verra / Gold Standard reference.

title: cer 0.1.0
text: cer_cache_info(): inspect local cache. cer_clear_cache(): clear locally cached files.

title: cer 0.1.0
text: offsets-integrity-primer: Chubb Review context integrity-weighted aggregation. safeguard-reform: pre- vs post-2023 regime handling. nger-scope-reconciliation: Scope 1 / 2 discipline. ret-compliance-primer: LGC / SRES primer. methodology: hand-computed worked examples.

title: cer 0.1.0
text: Data published Clean Energy Regulator https://cer.gov.au/markets/reports--data Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International licence. downloads cached locally first use SHA-256 integrity verification.
